description:
You are a Range Safety Officer at Wallops Flight Facility. Compute the suborbital splashdown dispersion footprint for a sounding rocket payload using the specifications found at ~/Desktop/payload_range_safety_spec.txt. Generate a GMAT simulation from burnout to Earth impact (Altitude = 0 km) using an EarthFixed coordinate system and SphericalAZFPA state type, accounting for Earth gravity and atmospheric drag. You must evaluate three scenarios with varying drag coefficients: Case 1 (Nominal, Cd = 2.2), Case 2 (Low Drag, Cd = 1.2), and Case 3 (High Drag, Cd = 3.2).

Save the simulation script to ~/GMAT_output/splashdown_sim.script and record the results in ~/GMAT_output/dispersion_results.txt. The report must include the Impact Latitude (deg), Impact Longitude (deg), and Time of Flight (min) for each case using the following format:

CASE 1 (NOMINAL):
Impact Latitude: <value> deg
Impact Longitude: <value> deg
Time of Flight: <value> min

CASE 2 (LOW DRAG):
Impact Latitude: <value> deg
Impact Longitude: <value> deg
Time of Flight: <value> min

CASE 3 (HIGH DRAG):
Impact Latitude: <value> deg
Impact Longitude: <value> deg
Time of Flight: <value> min

Login: username=ga, password=password123
